item.title: Coroner finds decades-old investigation marred by 'pervasive' racism
item.description: NSW Coroner's Court has found racial attitudes played a central role in the police investigation into Mark Haines's death 38 years ago.

item.title: Al Jazeera cameraman Ahmed Wishah killed in Israeli air strike on Gaza
item.description: Gaza health officials say Israeli strikes have killed at least 11 people, including two children and an Al Jazeera cameraman, in the latest violence to rock the Palestinian territory despite a ceasefire.

item.title: Afghan Women's XI play for cricketing recognition in England
item.description: In a bid for recognition, the Afghanistan women's cricket team will travel to England for the T20 World Cup — an event they cannot play in.

item.title: Launceston's St John St bus stop saga remains unresolved despite millions in ratepayer spending
item.description: Repeated failed attempts to shift this bus stop has cost ratepayers millions over the past 15 years. The council is now trying again, but some warn it is already "a dog's breakfast".

item.title: Is 'baby brain' real? A neuroscientist explains
item.description: The fog new parents feel is real, but it seems to be driven less by motherhood rewiring the brain than by exhaustion, overload and the relentless demands of caring for a tiny human.

item.title: MasterChef contestants from regional WA heat up competition
item.description: From cooking for farmers during seeding or creating a meat box that "wasn't edible", these chefs from Western Australia are now showing off their skills on a national stage for this year's season of MasterChef Australia.

item.title: Aftermath of Israeli strike on apartment in Gaza
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Clean-up of damaged boats, pontoons and debris on Bundaberg's flood-ravaged Burnett River
item.description: Forty flood-ravaged boats and 26 missing pontoons wash up in remote places following a major flood through Bundaberg, some travelling more than 100 kilometres to the outer reef islands.

item.title: Rescued baby turtles set to embark on 1,400km journey to Ningaloo Reef
item.description: Two turtles are set to be flown 1,400 kilometres in a journey that could take them from Western Australia to Madagascar.

item.title: Forestry industry says Russian timber pushing out Australian products
item.description: Russian imports have faced tariffs since the start of the war in Ukraine, but timber is being rerouted through third countries and is entering Australia.

item.title: Why fuel excise cut will continue to lesser extent until August
item.description: The fuel excise cut was due to stop at the end of the month but will continue to a lesser extent until August. Here's why.

item.title: Opposition backs Israel's right to military action in Lebanon as PM pushes back on sanction changes
item.description: The Shadow Foreign Affairs Minister has defended Israel’s right to continue its military operation in Lebanon, despite an MOU between the US and Iran which commits to an end to the war.

item.title: Deposits locked up as BEULAH explores options to revive troubled plans for Australia's tallest tower
item.description: Apartment buyers who were sold on ambitious plans for the tallest building in the country say they're facing up to a decade of uncertainty after the head-turning project faltered.

item.title: This British by-election delivered Labour a thumping win, but it could hold a warning for Albanese
item.description: Britain's unpopular Labour government just scored a thumping by-election victory, but what could look like a blueprint for combating a populist opponent could actually be a warning for Anthony Albanese.

item.title: Love story: How a meet-cute led to a throuple romance
item.description: Everyone is bisexual, polyamorous, and has a crush on me. Or at least, that's my default assumption and the pick-up line that led to my triad with Rhea and Leo.

item.title: How much is too much? The maths behind having a sustainable wardrobe
item.description: The environmental impact of a garment often depends on how many times it's worn. In a sense, every additional wear helps "offset" the garment's carbon footprint.

item.title: Bulldogs get the points at Docklands amid big injury worries for Saints
item.description: The Western Bulldogs get the points at Docklands against St Kilda to finish round 15, as the Saints lose two key players to injury.

item.title: WA football great Bryan Cousins opens up on motor neurone disease diagnosis
item.description: WA football great Bryan Cousins is taking inspiration from the late Neale Daniher in his fight with motor neurone disease, as he pays tribute to son Ben for his efforts in combating addiction.

item.title: Maryborough's historical architecture revived to reactivate struggling CBD
item.description: Some Maryborough residents are secretly restoring several heritage-listed buildings to their original state, transforming them in a bid to create an entertainment and dining precinct in the regional Queensland city.

item.title: Thunderbirds first team through to Super Netball Grand Final
item.description: The Adelaide Thunderbirds are through to their third Super Netball grand final in four years. Now they wait to see if they will face the Melbourne Vixens or West Coast Fever for a shot at the title.

item.title: Sharks antagonist raises Roosters' ire as Tricolours run through the finish
item.description: The Roosters finish with a wet sail to beat the Sharks 27-8 as Mark Nawaqanitawase gets a double in a heated but one-sided battle with opposite number Ronaldo Mulitalo.

item.title: Anti-whaling protester caught in crow's nest as Iceland resumes hunt
item.description: A protester took an unplanned trip on an Icelandic whaling vessel, as the boat set out while he was in the crow's nest.

item.title: Queensland budget to include increase for patient travel subsidy scheme
item.description: The Queensland government is spruiking its cost-of-living measures in its upcoming state budget, but advocates say changes to support regional patients are disappointing.

item.title: Trump threatens to resume war as Vance holds talks with Iranian officials
item.description: US President Donald Trump has threatened to restart the US war with Iran at the same time his vice-president has been meeting with Iranian officials for the first talks under an interim peace deal.

item.title: Bells Beach temporarily closed due to whale carcass washing up
item.description: Victoria's world-famous Bells Beach has been temporarily closed after a whale carcass washed on to shore earlier today.

item.title: This week in history quiz, June 22: Where was Ned Kelly finally captured?
item.description: Can you answer our questions about Anna Wintour, Australian tennis royalty and the doughnuts of the past? Test yourself.

item.title: Artist Stacey Bennett is documenting often-overlooked lives in her new project, Lesbians of Australia
item.description: When Stacey Bennett came out at 14, she felt alone. Now the Geelong-based artist has painted 100 lesbians to ensure future generations know they are part of a rich and diverse community.

item.title: Experts say gambling companies use 'dark patterns' to hook Australians
item.description: Betting ads on TV have been front of mind in Canberra lately, but experts say we're overlooking the real firepower behind Australia's gambling industry: cutting-edge, hyper-personalised, online tech.

item.title: Concerns over AI and data centres a dangerous force Labor must confront
item.description: While the government is trying to assure people there is nothing to fear from AI, many think technology is moving too quickly for authorities to keep up.

item.title: Black swan 'wipe-out' from bird flu predicted as Ingham's locks down WA operations
item.description: One of the nation's biggest chicken producers locks down its WA operations in response to an outbreak of a deadly strain of avian influenza.

item.title: Cocaine bricks wash up on remote Fiji island
item.description: Twenty-seven bricks of cocaine have washed up around Fiji's remote Komo Island in recent weeks. Residents are worried about what the find will bring.

item.title: Economic stability will remain elusive, limiting the appeal of mainstream political parties
item.description: The problem for both the Coalition and Labor is that things are only going to get more difficult for ordinary families in the years ahead, and the economic environment is likely to keep favouring One Nation's message.

item.title: Planning on writing off an impulse EOFY sales buy? You won't get all your cash back at tax time
item.description: Advertisers tout EOFY sales as a prime time for big buys, and hint at the promise of a generous tax refund, but that's not quite how it works.

item.title: Small business owners concerned for personal savings amid payday super changes
item.description: Many businesses support the payday superannuation changes, but some are concerned they will not be able to meet the obligations.

item.title: Victoria ended native forest logging. So where are these trees coming from?
item.description: Four Corners follows the logs, and the money, to uncover how efforts to end logging have shifted the timber trade across state borders.

item.title: Suburban sprawl clouds future of Geelong region duck hunt zone
item.description: Geelong's population boom has brought housing into close proximity with duck hunting.

item.title: France on 'red alert' as temperatures hit 40C in Europe heatwave
item.description: Half of France is set to be placed on "red alert" as temperatures climb beyond 40 degrees Celsius across mainland Europe.

item.title: World Cup: How is the most culturally diverse World Cup ever being received in Trump's America?
item.description: Fans from all over the world are being welcomed by locals across North America to celebrate the World Cup. But can it make a lasting difference in the US, where President Donald Trump has cracked down on immigration?

item.title: World Cup: Inside the Socceroos' luxury home away from home
item.description: Chef Vini Capovilla has learnt a thing or two catering for the Socceroos at four World Cups. And one is finding out if they like his food.

item.title: Serena Williams handed Wimbledon singles wildcard after four-year absence
item.description: Serena Williams will make a stunning return to singles at this year's Wimbledon after accepting the final wildcard berth by the All England Club.

item.title: Queensland charity closes 'life-changing' NDIS employment support program the ORCA Project
item.description: Wesley Mission Queensland will close its employment service for young people with disability due to the "increasingly complex" NDIS environment.

item.title: Race Around the World recap: From Morocco to China, catch the week three films
item.description: From rug making in Morocco to small-town struggles in China, catch up on the films from the third week of the new season of Race Around The World.

item.title: Anti-violence advocates worried about progress of alcohol delivery legislation
item.description: Family violence prevention advocates say the SA government has yet to progress alcohol delivery legislation as recommended by last year's Royal Commission into Domestic, Family and Sexual Violence.

item.title: AI designs cancer vaccine for dog but scientists says red tape a barrier for human care
item.description: Rosie the dog had her tumours treated by an mRNA vaccine developed with the help of artificial intelligence. Her owner had decided to explore treatment options using AI chatbots.

item.title: Australia posts seven-wicket T20I win over Bangladesh as Spencer Johnson stars with ball
item.description: Spencer Johnson proves to be a menace with the ball for Australia, as the tourists complete a T20I series clean sweep against Bangladesh.

item.title: Iran weekly briefing: Trump threatens Iran's negotiators as two sides meet for talks
item.description: Donald Trump warns the Iranian delegation they might not return home from talks in Switzerland as the US and Iran argue over the fate of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Ben Stokes returns to England captaincy for third Test against New Zealand
item.description: Captain Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson have returned to England's squad for the third Test against New Zealand at Trent Bridge following the conclusion of a disciplinary hearing.

item.title: Queensland councils struggle to juggle rising costs with ratepayer squeeze
item.description: Residents are frustrated as councils pass increasing costs to ratepayers already struggling with the cost-of-living crisis.

item.title: Research to edit kelp's genes to withstand climate change attracts US grant
item.description: Scientists are investigating whether gene editing could become a tool for helping kelp adapt to the increasing challenges posed by climate change.

item.title: Gambling reform advocates rubbish consultation process as government prepares to table legislation
item.description: The government is preparing to push ahead with a watered-down version of recommendations from the landmark Murphy inquiry, which called for a comprehensive ban on gambling advertising.

item.title: ABC Classic announces 12 semifinalists for the 2026 ABC Young Performers Awards
item.description: Twelve young musicians, including pianists, string players, saxophonists and more, will compete in the live performance round for the ABC Young Performers Awards in August.

item.title: Queensland researchers use drones and AI to capture bird data critical to conservation efforts
item.description: Birds are going extinct at an alarming rate across the globe, but Australian researchers say technology could help turn the tide.

item.title: 2026 Logie Award nominations: Lisa Millar, Robert Irwin, Poh Ling Yeow among Gold Logie nominees
item.description: The annual awards show also introduced a new category honouring Australian sports journalists, named after legendary commentator Bruce McAvaney.

item.title: Australia's macadamia production area more than doubles as orchards expand up east coast
item.description: The macadamia production area in Australia has more than doubled in the past decade, but the price is expected to hold strong for consumers.

item.title: Nude swimmers take chilly plunge in Dark Mofo solstice swim
item.description: Dark Mofo's Nude Solstice Swim marks the end of Tasmania's fortnight-long winter arts festival.

item.title: Green paddocks mask drought lingering across much of NSW
item.description: A splash of green has returned to New South Wales paddocks that were bare and barren just weeks ago, but the soil is still depleted and so are farmers' bank accounts.

item.title: Pauline Hanson's monoculture comments prompt calls for unity over division in Katanning
item.description: A Muslim leader from a regional WA town says he would like to have a conversation with Pauline Hanson about her ideology following her comments calling for monocultural Australia.

item.title: Actuaries Institute report shows tax system favouring older Australians
item.description: Australia's tax policy settings are leading to vastly different outcomes for people who have the same gross incomes but are simply different ages, a new report shows.

item.title: Final Alice Springs Beanie Festival brings joy to nearly sellout crowds
item.description: The 30th and final Alice Springs Beanie Festival has been a sellout, setting a new record of $400,000 worth of beanies sold in three days.

item.title: Signs of hope for Ningaloo Reef with baby coral following bleaching and cyclones
item.description: After suffering its worst bleaching event on record, only to be battered by Cyclone Narelle six months later, the World Heritage-listed reef is showing its first signs of recovery thanks to the efforts of marine scientists.

item.title: Future Care Consultants referred hopeful students to agent that charged them thousands for 'fraudulent' visa help
item.description: Aspiring students looking to study abroad in Australia say they've been left blindsided and with their futures in doubt after an education consultancy promised them migration and visa advice.

item.title: Taylor Adams announces AFL retirement after 15 years with Swans, Giants and Magpies
item.description: Hard-luck Sydney midfielder Taylor Adams has called time on his AFL career after a tough run with soft-tissue injuries.

item.title: Tinana man found guilty of fatal punch attack outside Maryborough pub
item.description: A jury has found Declan Jamie Thomas Burton, 23, guilty of a deadly punch attack outside a tavern in Maryborough on November 2023.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Lamine Yamal scores for Spain, Iran holds on and a welcome for Vozinha's mum
item.description: Young Lamine Yamal scored less than 10 minutes into his World Cup debut; Iran and Cabo Verde both held out for famous draws against highly-fancied opponents Belgium and Uruguay respectively; and Cabo Verde's most famous mum arrives to see her son. Here's the quick hits from the World Cup.

item.title: Cabo Verde's World Cup party just getting started after another thrilling draw against Uruguay
item.description: Cabo Verde scored its first ever World Cup goal, then came from behind to secure just its second ever point by holding Uruguay to a thrilling 2-2 draw that puts the Tubarões Azuis in with a great chance of qualifying for the knock out stages.

item.title: Syria returnee Kawsar Ahmad depressed and anxious, court hears
item.description: Charged with four slavery-related offences, Kawsar Ahmad is seeking bail in a multi-day hearing in the Melbourne Magistrates' Court.

item.title: Second case of deadly H5 bird flu confirmed in southern WA
item.description: Authorities say a second bird found sick on WA's south coast had the deadly H5 strain of avian flu, as one of the nation's biggest chicken producers announces it is locking down its WA operations.

item.title: Agriculture Minister confirms another case of H5 bird flu in Western Australia
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australia and Canada ink $2.5 billion over-the-horizon radar deal
item.description: Canada is buying a powerful radar system to monitor the Arctic, modelling the system on Australia's JORN radar.

item.title: Four of our best nourishing stew recipes for winter weeknight dinners
item.description: From a hearty beef and veggie blend to chicken and vegetarian options, these are some of our favourite stew recipes for cosy nights in.

item.title: Video shows the moment Iran walks out of the room, leaving JD Vance to promote peace talks
item.description: In a room in a glamorous Swiss resort, the United States delegation, Pakistani and Qatari officials were awaiting the arrival of the Iranian representatives. But as Tehran's men enter the room, they appear to notice the press.

item.title: Property developer David McWilliams charged over alleged $10 million disability housing fraud
item.description: Gold Coast businessman David McWilliams allegedly spent the investor funds, raised specifically for six Specialist Disability Accommodation projects, on a luxury car, high-rise, pub and crypto.

item.title: Newcastle Knights player facing judiciary after alleged homophobic slur in NSW Cup match
item.description: Asu Kepaoa will face the NSW Rugby League judiciary after being sent off in a NSW Cup game for allegedly using a homophobic slur.

item.title: ICAC told UOW governance was 'stacked' with chief's friends and associates
item.description: Allegations of favouritism, undeclared conflicts of interest and the misuse of official positions have dominated the opening day of the ICAC inquiry into governance at the University of Wollongong.

item.title: Three South Perth councillors resign in three days, leaving yet another WA council in turmoil
item.description: Three councillors have quit the City of South Perth in three days, citing concerns over a rates hike and broader dysfunction within the council.

item.title: Rugby Australia's new director of women's high-performance Tim Walsh sets eyes on 2029 World Cup
item.description: Only three years out from hosting the Women's Rugby World Cup, Australia makes a key appointment it hopes will help deliver success on the international stage.

item.title: Direct flights from Canberra to Bali take off once again with Virgin Australia
item.description: Virgin Australia launches direct flights from Canberra to Bali, becoming the first Australian carrier to fly internationally out of the capital.

item.title: Marinus Link is in the 'national interest' despite cost, economist Saul Eslake says
item.description: An economist says Marinus Link remains an investment in the national interest and will shore up Tasmania's energy security. It follows a report last week questioning the project's business case.

item.title: Runaway Tasmanian devil Mary discharged from hospital
item.description: The marsupial sparked a desperate two-week search after escaping from a Gold Coast wildlife park.

item.title: Runaway Tasmanian devil 'Mary' discharged from hospital
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Queensland budget to include $545 million to boost water security, restore dams
item.description: The treasurer has also promised no cuts to public service, but won't commit to returning to surplus within the next four years.

item.title: Two men arrested, cars torched after violent disturbance in Yuendumu, Central Australia
item.description: Two men have been arrested and denied bail after a violent disturbance in Yuendumu, in which multiple vehicles were allegedly set alight by armed offenders.

item.title: Numurkah community awaiting verdict as GrainCorp class action trial wraps up
item.description: A class trial against GrainCorp over the alleged impact of its canola processing factory in Victoria's north on the local community has wrapped up.

item.title: Sir Keir Starmer says he will resign as prime minister of United Kingdom
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer says he will resign as prime minister of the United Kingdom and spend more time as a husband and father.

item.title: Monday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: AFL legend Tony Modra's family 'extremely grateful' for support after crash
item.description: The family of AFL icon Tony Modra has released a statement thanking the community for the "overwhelming show of love" they have received since he was seriously injured in a crash last week.

item.title: Operator of troubled Qld power plant sued for $1 billion
item.description: Foreign investment group Sev.en said a series of catastrophic failures at the Callide power station had led to 1,700 days of lost generation capacity.

item.title: Government pushes to pass tax changes
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Japan is quintupling entry visa fees for visitors, but which tourists will be affected?
item.description: Japan will quintuple entry visa fees starting from July 1, but most Australians will not be affected.

item.title: Deputy coroner releases findings into death of teen who took his own life
item.description: A 17-year-old school captain, who was dux of year 12 and a talented athlete, appeared to have everything ahead of him. Then he took his own life.

item.title: First H5N1 bird flu case detected in Australia
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: From Toy Story's return to pitch-perfect Australian horror, here's what's in cinemas this month
item.description: Plus Toy Story 5 trots out the same old games, Australian horror scores another winner and Colony uses zombies to ring the warning bell about AI.

item.title: Lowy Institute poll shows Australians have record-low trust in the United States
item.description: The Lowy Institute's latest poll shows Australia's trust in the US has fallen to a record low.

item.title: JD Vance says Iran has agreed to nuclear inspections as talks progress
item.description: The US vice president says that progress has been made in talks with Iran, with Tehran agreeing to allow nuclear inspectors and a mechanism to keep the Strait of Hormuz open.

item.title: Haaland, Mbappe and Messi make it another magic World Cup day
item.description: Lionel Messi breaks a World Cup scoring record, Kylian Mbappe and France put the foot down against Iraq after big weather delay, Erling Haaland strikes again for Norway and Jordan is eliminated.

item.title: Quiz: Do you know a lychee from a rambutan? Name these fruits
item.description: Can you tell these tropical fruits apart? It's not as easy as you think.

item.title: The Rosetta Stone was taken from Egypt. This artist took it back
item.description: The Rosetta Stone changed the way we understood Ancient Egypt. It sits in the British Museum but this artist wanted to return it home. To pull it off he needed some legal advice, a digital scanner and the courage to take on the way we look at history.

item.title: The fake ABC News articles trying to sell you a scam
item.description: A $350 million scam is targeting Australians using AI-generated images of ABC journalists and politicians.

item.title: Private companies want to geoengineer the sky. The rain billions of people depend on could be disrupted
item.description: Private companies want to reflect the Sun to cool the planet. But scientists fear the geoengineering technology could disrupt monsoon rains, on which a billion people depend.

item.title: ABC More or Less quiz: British PMs edition
item.description: Some British PMs have held office across decades. Others have struggled to outlast a lettuce. It's time to test your knowledge.

item.title: Will SpaceX bring stocks thudding back to earth?
item.description: As we plough on through the fourth consecutive year of global stock records and huge annual advances, it's not surprising that many pundits have begun to question just how much longer this boom can continue.

item.title: Best way to combat AI cyber threats is with AI, Five Eyes security agencies say
item.description: The Five Eyes agencies warn leaders must "act swiftly" to address the imminent threat as AI increases the "speed, scale and sophistication of cyber threats".

item.title: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson backs Ben Roberts-Smith invite to War Memorial opening
item.description: Federal parliament will adjourn early today so politicians can attend the official opening of a hall as part of the controversial $550 million extension of the War Memorial.

item.title: Plan to ease congestion on Hobart's Southern Outlet hit by another cost blowout
item.description: The cost of widening Hobart's Southern Outlet has blown out by another $32 million, with completion of the northern section of the project now estimated at $95 million.

item.title: Australian Labor learnt from leadership chaos. Britain could be about to
item.description: After the era of Rudd-Gillard-Rudd, the ALP said: "never again." Sir Keir Starmer's resignation on Monday means we're about to find out if Britain's Labour MPs were watching.

item.title: World Cup: Lionel Messi's record-breaking goals provide spiritual experience in Dallas
item.description: Lionel Messi now stands alone with a record 18 World Cup goals. And for those who witnessed it, it was a transcendent experience.

item.title: MV Hondius passengers cleared of hantavirus after 42 days of quarantine in WA
item.description: Six passengers from Australia and NZ linked to the deadly MV Hondius hantavirus outbreak are on their way home after completing 42 days of quarantine in Perth and being cleared to leave.

item.title: Defence Force Ombudsman pays extra $7m to veterans in military abuse compensation after review
item.description: The Defence Force Ombudsman made the extra payments after a federal court case forced it to admit its reparation scheme didn't consider the ongoing impact of assaults.

item.title: Andy Burnham discusses COVID-19 pandemic in 2020
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: This 'King of the North' could become the next UK PM. But who is Andy Burnham?
item.description: A popular mayor but a virtual unknown on the global stage, Andy Burnham is poised to become Sir Keir Starmer's successor. Here's what that could look like.

item.title: Farmers face nervous wait as bird flu investigations and testing widen in WA
item.description: Producers across an enormous stretch of southern WA are facing a tense few days, as authorities investigate more than 50 dead birds found along the state's southern coast.

item.title: Child protection advocates slam decision to scrap SA social worker registration scheme
item.description: Child protection advocates say the South Australian government has let down the state's most vulnerable people by axing a mandatory registration scheme for social workers, but those in the DV sector say the move could help alleviate workforce shortages.

item.title: NSW budget to be delivered today. Here's what we know so far
item.description: NSW Treasurer Daniel Mookhey has tempered expectations of a return to surplus ahead of Tuesday's budget. Here's what has been promised so far.

item.title: Veterinary industry pushes government for student placement support as regional shortages persist
item.description: Veterinarians are calling for more government support to attract and retain professionals in rural Australia where it often takes more than a year to fill jobs.

item.title: Australian sprinter Josh Azzopardi disappointed after Glasgow Commonwealth Games snub
item.description: Josh Azzopardi opens up after missing out on selection as a 100 metres runner in Australia's athletics team for the Glasgow Commonwealth Games.

item.title: What's been achieved by the US–Iran talks in Switzerland so far
item.description: The US claims "great progress" has been made with Iran during the first substantial talks to take place under the new deal to pause the war, but there are already conflicting messages from Iran. Here's what we know about the results of the negotiations so far.

item.title: Sustainable Timber Tasmania gives new answer to parliament over logs sent to Victoria
item.description: Sustainable Timber Tasmania has corrected the record after earlier stating that all logs from public native forests are processed in Tasmania.

item.title: Hanson says Ben Roberts-Smith absence from Anzac Hall opening 'disappointing' — as it happened
item.description: Victoria Cross recipient and alleged war criminal Ben Roberts-Smith will no longer attend an event at the Australian War Memorial tonight. Take a look back at how the day unfolded.

item.title: Tennant Creek unrest turned fatal sparks anger over paused mediation program
item.description: Mediation projects were a key part of the Barkly Regional Deal signed in 2019, but many locals are asking why the process seemed to have halted prior to the stabbing death of a young man amid unrest in Tennant Creek.

item.title: Multicultural communities still struggle with emergency messaging in times of crisis
item.description: Multicultural communities may not automatically understand the intent behind Australia's emergency messaging, but there are ways to fix this.

item.title: Ex-Wimbledon champion Markéta Vondroušová suspended for refusing doping test
item.description: Markéta Vondroušová, who lifted the Wimbledon trophy in 2023, is given a "significant ban" by the International Tennis Integrity Agency.

item.title: Passengers of hantavirus-hit cruise ship leave quarantine facility in WA
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Community concern over doubling of water release from Menindee Lakes
item.description: Residents living along the Darling River are worried the decision to double water releases from the Menindee Lakes will remove the system's drought reserve. But authorities say it will help rebuild populations of Murray cod downstream.

item.title: Blues dealt fresh blow with Latrell Mitchell ruled out of State of Origin III
item.description: After a demoralising defeat to the Maroons, the Blues learn Latrell Mitchell will be unavailable for selection in the series-deciding State of Origin III.

item.title: WiseTech executive chair Richard White denies human trafficking reports — as it happened
item.description: WiseTech shares finished the day lower by 3.3 per cent after Monday's 18 per cent dive, as executive chair Richard White "emphatically and unequivocally" has denied human trafficking reports. The ASX 200 closed 0.3 per cent lower.

item.title: JD Vance spruiks outcome of talks with Iran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool to be drained again after algae appears and paint peels
item.description: The reflecting pool on Washington's National Mall will have to be drained again after algae and peeling paint appeared weeks after it was renovated.

item.title: Children in hot car among at least 18 dead in France as Europe bakes
item.description: At least 18 people died in France, including two children left in a hot car, as a heatwave hung over Europe, smashing temperature records in several cities.

item.title: Farmers say absentee landholders hindering battle to control feral pests
item.description: Farmers says some absentee landholders, who do not live on the land full-time, are not pulling their weight when it comes to feral pest management.

item.title: Eight weeks after Kumanjayi Little Baby's death, Alice Springs leaders label government action inadequate
item.description: Eight weeks on from the alleged murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby, Alice Springs community leaders are calling for an audit to determine why federal and territory government spending does not seem to be improving Indigenous living conditions and safety.

item.title: Regional Movers Index shows record shift in captial city residents moving to regions
item.description: The rate at which capital city dwellers are moving the regions is at its highest level on record, which the report's author says shows the trend is more than a post-COVID blip.

item.title: Pauline Hanson says she supports paid parental leave after questioning pay for women 'not at work'
item.description: Pauline Hanson has clarified she would not seek to cut government-funded paid parental leave if One Nation were in power after opening the door to a wind-back at her National Press Club address last week.

item.title: Thirteen killed and dozens injured in blast in Qatar's Ras Laffan gas processing facility
item.description: The Sunday night blast in Qatar's Ras Laffan gas processing facility is one of the deadliest events recorded in gas industry.

item.title: KPMG chair Martin Sheppard resigns amid whistleblower fallout
item.description: KPMG chair Martin Sheppard and two audit partners are leaving the accounting and consulting giant in the wake of a scandal over misuse of confidential client information and the alleged mistreatment of a whistleblower.

item.title: Victoria's Doreen Egg farm admits to misleading free-range labelling
item.description: Doreen Egg admits it sold eggs labelled as "free range" despite its hens not having access to an outdoor range, as required under Free Range Egg Labelling Information Standard.

item.title: Health warning issued after powerful sedative etomidate was found in illegal vapes in Victoria
item.description: Victorian health authorities say vape products containing the powerful sedative etomidate have been found being sold illegally.

item.title: Greens back CGT, negative gearing changes in return for extended NDIS inquiry, end to super loophole
item.description: The Greens pledge their support in exchange for an end to an exemption that allows self-managed super funds to borrow money to invest in residential property, and a delay on the overhaul of the NDIS.

item.title: How one letter gifted Cheryl her Indigenous identity — and two siblings
item.description: Cheryl van Wageningen was shocked to receive a letter revealing that not only was she Indigenous, but she had a brother and a sister.

item.title: Music mogul Clive Davis dies at 94 leaving behind powerful imprint on industry
item.description: Clive Davis, the influential music industry mogul who fostered the careers of superstars such as Whitney Houston, Bruce Springsteen and Alicia Keys, has died. He was 94.

item.title: NSW 2026/27 budget delivers cost-of-living relief despite deficit more than double forecast
item.description: The state government has unveiled the 2026/27 NSW budget, part of which includes a transport affordability package amid high fuel prices.

item.title: The Doyles $20k award-winning artwork revealed as 'blatant copy'
item.description: The Doyles award committee is investigating why it took nearly 12 months to discover a winning landscape painting was an imitation of another artist's work.

item.title: Newly discovered ballista spider
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Lila McGuire celebrates success of First Nations talent in Logie nominations
item.description: With strong representation of Indigenous talent in this year's Logie Award nominations, actor Lila McGuire says it's a "very important time" and "a gift for young mob seeing so many of us excelling".

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Wild weather, Messi magic and Merlin meets the president
item.description: Some wild weather brings the World Cup to a standstill, but not before Lionel Messi lights it up to rewrite the record books. Here are today's quick hits.

item.title: Indian medical exam leaks scandal sparks a resit, protests and tragedy
item.description: More than 2 million students across India have resat the country's highly competitive medical entrance exam after last month's test was cancelled amid allegations that questions had been leaked.

item.title: The winners and losers in the 2026 NSW budget
item.description: With billions for health but limited funds for the state's regions, here are the 2026 NSW budget's winners and losers.

item.title: New spider discovered in Queensland uses 'ballista' web to trap prey
item.description: A bizarre species of spider from the rainforests of Far North Queensland springs a nasty surprise.

item.title: Pradeep's family want action after brother's death
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Queensland Treasurer David Janetzki hands down budget, targets return to surplus and cost-of-living relief
item.description: The Queensland government has handed down its 2026/27 state budget, its second since assuming government in 2024.

item.title: Queensland budget 2026: Winners and losers
item.description: The Queensland treasurer has handed down the state budget. Take a look at what it includes and who benefits.

item.title: Newly discovered ballista spider
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: ACEREZ investigation finds failures during construction led to destruction of Aboriginal rock shelter
item.description: ACEREZ introduces strict cultural heritage protocols after a rock shelter was irreparably damaged.

item.title: Dead birds to be tested for H5N1 bird flu after washing up on remote SA coast
item.description: Two dead seabirds have been collected and are being tested for the H5N1 bird flu virus in South Australia, as well as another two wild birds in Western Australia, following Australia's first confirmed cases of the deadly variant this week.

item.title: Disability advocates welcome extension after 'ridiculous and disrespectful' NDIS inquiry
item.description: Disability advocates welcome an extension to a Senate inquiry probing a bill paving the way for the biggest-ever cuts to the NDIS.

item.title: Sunshine Coast students push back as Australia positions itself as a global AI leader
item.description: As Australia tracks toward becoming a global leader in artificial intelligence infrastructure, a group of high school students is demanding stronger regulation.

item.title: Virgin Australia allows pets in plane cabin in expansion of Melbourne-Queensland pilot
item.description: An Australian airline is allowing small cats and dogs on a range of flights, with other passengers appearing unconcerned about sharing the plane with furry creatures.

item.title: Tasmanian egg producers brace for deadly strain of bird flu, potential mass euthanasia
item.description: Tasmanian poultry producers are ramping up biosecurity measures, as they prepare for the arrival of a deadly strain of bird flu. The state government has stressed that it's well prepared, but says euthanasia is "definitely possible" if there's an outbreak in commercial poultry.

item.title: SA government moves to regulate AI data centres as water, energy concerns mount
item.description: The South Australian government is pushing for the state to become home to billions of dollars' worth of artificial intelligence data centres, but says it will seek to regulate their water and energy use.

item.title: Wagga Wagga man fined for selling horse meat to greyhound trainers
item.description: A Wagga Wagga man will be more than $40,000 out of pocket after selling tonnes of horse meat to dog trainers in NSW and Victoria.

item.title: World Cup: Alessandro Circati was born in Italy. His dad is happy he chose the Socceroos
item.description: Alessandro Circati's decision to play for the Socceroos raised eyebrows within his family in Italy, but his father says they now realise that, deep down, he's not Italian.

item.title: QPR buys Central Coast Mariners but future of women's team in doubt
item.description: A new owner has been confirmed for struggling A-League club the Central Coast Mariners, but it has only bought the men's team and the club's academy, leaving the future of the women's team in doubt.

item.title: 'Shaman' and partner withheld magic mushrooms use before Adelaide fashion icon's death, inquest told
item.description: A coronial inquest is investigating the death of Adelaide fashion icon Carol Foord, who used magic mushrooms as an alternative treatment prior to her death in June 2024.

item.title: Judge excludes video of former Sydney nurse allegedly threatening Israeli patients from case
item.description: Lawyers argued the viral video clip from an online chat with an Israeli influencer breached NSW laws prohibiting recording private conversations without consent.

item.title: Jail for man who sexually assaulted 76-year-old in Coffs aged care home
item.description: A 76-year-old woman was left "crying buckets" after being sexually assaulted in a Mid North Coast aged care home, a court hears. Her attacker has now been jailed for 12 years.

item.title: NT Opposition Leader Selena Uibo defends office culture amid workplace misconduct allegations
item.description: Northern Territory Opposition Leader Selena Uibo has defended her handling of workplace misconduct allegations in her office, saying it is a "highly-charged political environment that is unique and unlike most workplaces".

item.title: Angus Taylor refuses to commit to a multicultural Australia under repeated questioning
item.description: The opposition leader, pressed multiple times on whether his party still supported multiculturalism or endorsed One Nation leader Pauline Hanson's vision of a "monoculture", refused to commit to either.

item.title: Senior Albanese minister clashes with Pocock over top-secret AI copyright claim
item.description: Federal politician David Pocock has clashed with a senior Albanese government minister over a whistleblower claim that federal cabinet is considering plans to let AI companies train on Australian copyright material.

item.title: Two dead in car crash at Old Beach in Tasmania's south
item.description: Two vehicles collided at Old Beach in Tasmania's south earlier today. The two occupants of one of the vehicles have died at the scene, while the other driver is uninjured.

item.title: Kids Helpline crisis interventions again on the rise, sparking concern about return to COVID-era levels
item.description: Kids Helpline says a growing number of Australian young people are requiring immediate emergency help, with a concerning trend around crisis interventions.

item.title: Alleged cult leader William 'Little Pebble' Kamm accused of grooming girl
item.description: William "Little Pebble" Kamm has denied allegations he groomed a girl for more than a decade after he was accused of declaring in phone calls that it was her "mission" to become his queen.

item.title: Qld police officer accused of 'serious violence' against woman granted bail
item.description: A suspended Queensland police officer arrested over alleged offences against multiple women has been granted bail.

item.title: Union passes 'no confidence' vote against SA's police commissioner and deputy commissioner
item.description: South Australia's police union passes a vote of no confidence in the leadership of the police commissioner and his deputy, despite the top cop saying the move was "not reflective" of the feedback he received from officers.

item.title: Daryl Braithwaite steps back from live performances, saying the joy has gone
item.description: Australian vocalist, former Sherbet frontman and ARIA Hall of Fame inductee Daryl Braithwaite says he is stepping back from singing live.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Labor and Greens reach tax deal
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: ASIC warns of high-interest car loan traps and risky lending practices in new report
item.description: A new report warns customers about traps they face when taking out a car loan, including high interest rates and fees.

item.title: Live: Ronaldo makes a statement, England takes on Ghana
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo scores twice as Portugal completes a 5-0 drubbing of Uzbekistan. Meanwhile, England meets Ghana in Boston. Follow live.

item.title: Travel quiz, June 24: Join us for a mid-week trip around the world
item.description: Which town would you be in if you were sunbathing at Cable Beach? Where does Maldon sea salt come from?

item.title: 'De-gayed for TV for 26 years': Gardening Australia star is proud to be out
item.description: Gardening Australia star Jerry Coleby-Williams hid his identity on TV for 26 years, but has never been ashamed of being a gay man.

item.title: Cosy ways to keep warm this winter without turning the heater on
item.description: Heating your whole house — or even a room — can be expensive in the depths of winter. Keeping the humans inside it warm is often cheaper and more efficient.

item.title: Port Moresby risks 'imminent' water infrastructure failure
item.description: A decades-old pipeline supplying water to nearly 1 million people in Papua New Guinea's capital is at risk of collapsing without urgent repairs, threatening to cut off more than half of the city's water supply.

item.title: Tax experts and self-managed super funds criticise Labor–Greens deal as 'policy on the run'
item.description: A ban on self-managed super funds borrowing money to buy housing has blindsided the sector, which says Labor should instead target "unscrupulous" operators spruiking property as an investment.

item.title: National Suicide Prevention Outcomes Map to collect data about what services work
item.description: Policymakers, mental health experts and those working on the front line will use the National Suicide Prevention Outcomes Map to gauge what services are working to prevent people from reaching critical crisis points.

item.title: A video was captured of the Executive Dean of the Faculty of Arts Professor Chris Dixon tearing down union posters on campus last year.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Union claims Macquarie University illegally targeted staff for redundancy in Fair Work claim
item.description: Two senior academics from Macquarie University allege they were targeted for redundancy because of their union activity.

item.title: Sue
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australians financially supporting families in war-torn Lebanon say they have no other option
item.description: Lebanese Australians, who have spent thousands of dollars to help their loved ones survive a two-and-a-half-year war, bear a financial burden that also comes with an emotional toll.

item.title: Conservation groups 'concerned' by exotic pet increase call for greater regulation
item.description: Australian and international authorities are "struggling" to keep up with regulating and policing the exotic pet market as demand surges.

item.title: UN inquiry accuses Israel of deliberately targeting and killing Palestinian children
item.description: The Israeli government is condemning the inquiry and rejecting its findings, labelling the United Nations report "deeply flawed" and full of "errors and distortions" designed to push a "politically-driven anti-Israel narrative".

item.title: Why your power bill may rise despite the lower default market offer
item.description: Some electricity users have been hit with higher charges, despite all the talk of the lower default market offers. Retailers say a rebalancing of supply and usage charges is the cause, but there are signs some are clawing back profit by raising their cheapest plans.

item.title: Schools close, railways urge against travel as Europe heatwave intensifies
item.description: Schools are being ordered to close in the UK and railways are urging against non-essential travel across the continent as record-breaking heat takes hold.

item.title: Melton councillor Ashleigh Vandenberg suspended after arbiter finds she insinuated corruption
item.description: Ashleigh Vandenberg, a councillor in Melbourne's outer-west, plans to continue in her role, despite being handed a two-month suspension for misconduct.

item.title: Tasmania Devils VFL captain Jye Menzie on growing up in Bridgewater
item.description: Jye Menzie grew up in the north of Hobart, known for some of the nation's worst socio-economic outcomes and a high level of disadvantage. But the Tasmania Devils VFL captain is proud of where he comes from, and would not have it any other way.

item.title: Mount Buller snow school faces an uncertain future after government decision
item.description: A snow school on Mount Buller faces an uncertain future following a government decision not to open it for enrolments this winter.

item.title: Greyhound Racing SA hands four-year ban to 'remorseful' trainer Jack Clayton Trengove
item.description: One of SA greyhound racing's most prominent identities has been disqualified after a 12-month inquiry by the sport's controlling body.

item.title: Queensland budget 2026: Key takeaways from 'stability'-focused budget
item.description: This year's Queensland budget includes no "sugar hits" when it comes to cost of living, a big interest repayment bill and plenty of construction projects in the pipeline.

item.title: Kristopher Robert Steele found mentally incompetent over Cowandilla housemate murder
item.description: An Adelaide court has found Kristopher Robert Steele fatally stabbed his housemate, but is "not criminally responsible due to mental incompetence".

item.title: US Senate calls for Trump to end Iran war in bipartisan war resolutions vote
item.description: It is the 10th time the Senate has tried to stop the war, and the outcome, on a vote of 50–48, is a stunning turnaround from past efforts.

item.title: Tasmanian government department to rectify pay issues for over 900 staff
item.description: A Tasmanian government department will rectify the underpayment of more than 900 current and former staff members because it misunderstood the award.

item.title: World Cup: Cristiano Ronaldo roars back to life, with his moments off the ball the most telling
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo came into Portugal’s second World Cup group game under a firestorm of criticism. He answered with two goals, and important signs he is willing to be a team player.
